zh.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage於1490年出生在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特。她於大約1510年與Sir John Robinson在英國英格蘭米德萊可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1541年在英國英格蘭米德萊可享年51歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特聖 瑪格麗特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage于1490年出生在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特。她于大约1510年与Sir John Robinson在英国英格兰米德萊可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1541年在英国英格兰米德萊可享年51岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特聖 瑪格麗特。

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: Anne
meanings: A name corrpted from Hannah, q.v. It is also found several times in a male name. From Ann, Anne, we have as nicknames Nan and Nancy

variants.name: Nancy
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nan
meanings: Originally a pet form of Ann (for the initial N-, compare Ned ). It is now also widely used as a short form of Nancy .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage was born in 1490, in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom. She married Sir John Robinson about 1510, in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1541, in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 51, and was buried in St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage est née en 1490, à Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir John Robinson environ 1510, à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1541, à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage wurde 1490 in Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1510 in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom Sir John Robinson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1541 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage è nata il nel 1490 nel Westminster, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Sir John Robinson circa 1510, nel Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1541, nel Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savageは1490年にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターで生まれた。約1510年にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスでSir John Robinsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1541年にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスで51歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスタースト マーガレットにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage(은)는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1510년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에서 Sir John Robinson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1541년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 스트 마거릿에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage nasceu em 1490, em Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Sir John Robinson aproximadamente 1510, em Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1541, em Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage родилась в 1490 г. в местности вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Robinson около 1510 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1541 г. в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности ст маргарет, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Anne Agnes Savage nació en 1490, en Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Sir John Robinson aproximadamente 1510, en Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1541, en Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
